title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery Queen deduces the true identities of the corpses involved in the recent murders. By identifying a specific mole on Brad's thigh and a hernia previously diagnosed by Dr. Temple, Ellery confirms that the bodies of Brad and Meagal are indeed themselves. He further determines that the corpse hanging from the mast of the *Helen* was not Croscot, as Van would have claimed self-defense if innocent; instead, the body is Clinton, whom Van falsely claimed died seven months ago at the Arojo intersection. Ellery realizes that Croscot was actually the person killed at Arojo, meaning the "devil" the police had been seeking for months was already dead.

Ellery identifies Van as the killer of Croscot and eliminates other suspects: Meagal is ruled out due to geography, Brad due to an alibi involving a chess champion, and Kline due to his low intelligence and lack of understanding regarding the "T" symbol. Ellery reconstructs the plot, noting that Van (using the alias Fagan or Fann) lured Crosetti to Arojo via an anonymous letter. Crosetti, seeking revenge, used a car from Crocker to reach the crossroads but did not bring a suitcase, as he had no intention of dismembering a victim.

The mastermind, Vance (also known as Andrew Cullis-Tweed), orchestrated the murders to appear as Croscot's revenge. Vance used Clinton—whom he had hired from a Pittsburgh orphanage—to disguise his own identity. By arranging murders to look like the work of a fanatic, Vance intended to appear killed by an avenger while actually escaping with his life savings and money from his brother, Stephen. Ellery concludes that the decapitations and "T" symbols were not the work of a fanatic, but a calculated method to prevent the identification of the corpses by removing their heads.
